meaning: fate woven by the Norns
usage_example: Our wyrd was written long before this hour

meaning: personal fortune and soul-luck carried through lives
usage_example: Your hamingja shines bright today

meaning: the thinking mind and seat of emotion
usage_example: My hugr turns toward you like a compass to the north

meaning: primal layers of fate; debt owed to the universe
usage_example: The örlög laid down at birth cannot be unmade, only honored

meaning: the gift rune; reciprocity and sacred exchange
usage_example: Between us there is gebo — every gift given returns transformed

meaning: peace within the sacred enclosure; protective harmony
usage_example: Frith holds this hall together like the roots of Yggdrasil

meaning: the weaving magic of fate and perception
usage_example: She works seiðr with her breath and the turning of her thoughts

meaning: a seeress who walks between worlds
usage_example: The völva reads not only the present but the echo of all possible futures

meaning: toast — to the health and honor of all gathered
usage_example: Skál to the courage it takes to face each new dawn

meaning: mead — the sacred drink of poets and gods
usage_example: Words flow from the mouth like mjöðr from Oðin's stolen cask

meaning: the World Tree connecting all nine realms
usage_example: We are all leaves on Yggdrasil — separate yet one

meaning: spoken magic; rune-chanting to shape reality
usage_example: Every true word is a form of galdr

meaning: a poetic compound that names through metaphor
usage_example: The whale-road stretches before the longship — a kenning for the sea

meaning: a Norse poet who weaves truth into verse
usage_example: The skald's words outlast the warrior's blade

meaning: the three Norns who weave the web of wyrd
usage_example: The nornir cut no thread before its appointed hour

meaning: dream; vision sent through the veil of sleep
usage_example: The draumr was no idle wandering — it came with purpose

meaning: Oðin's raven of thought; memory that flies forward
usage_example: Huginn circles back with news the waking mind cannot gather

meaning: Oðin's raven of memory; what must not be forgotten
usage_example: Muninn carries the weight of all I have ever loved

meaning: inner strength and sacred power
usage_example: Draw on your megin when the path grows steep

meaning: power; the force that flows when will meets form
usage_example: þrúðr flows through deliberate hands

meaning: sacred lot-casting; the blood of fate-reading
usage_example: The hlaut was cast at dawn; the answer was clear to those who listened

meaning: enchanted; touched by the otherworld
usage_example: There is something galínn about the way the light falls here

meaning: slaying; the decisive moment of a final reckoning
usage_example: Not every víg is fought with iron

meaning: the sea-goddess who claims the drowned; wild taking
usage_example: Rán takes those the sea chooses — there is no argument

meaning: wealth and abundance — not merely material
usage_example: Auðr in friendship outlasts gold in the chest

meaning: the Vanadís — goddess of love, magic, war and death
usage_example: In the choice between battle and beauty, Freyja says: both

meaning: the rainbow bridge between Midgård and Ásgarðr
usage_example: Every threshold crossed is a bifröst in miniature

meaning: divine strength; the power that flows from the Aesir
usage_example: There is ásmegin in the act of choosing to do right

meaning: the sun-maiden who drives the solar chariot
usage_example: Sól does not rest — she simply continues her turning

meaning: the moon-man who pulls the tidal web
usage_example: Máni waxes and wanes, and so do I

meaning: night — the dark daughter who rides before dawn
usage_example: Nátt is not absence; she is presence of a deeper kind

meaning: day — the bright son who reveals what nátt obscures
usage_example: Each dagr is a gift not owed to us

meaning: fire — the sacred hearth force, cleansing and sustaining
usage_example: The eld burns low but does not die while breath remains

meaning: water — mirror, source, and destination
usage_example: Vatn remembers every stone it has passed over

meaning: wind — invisible will made audible
usage_example: The vindr speaks a language older than words

meaning: the earth — Thor's mother; the body of the world
usage_example: We are all children of jörð, no matter how high we climb

meaning: the Thunderer; protector of Midgård, champion of the folk
usage_example: þór does not defend because he must — he defends because that is who he is

meaning: the shape-changer; chaos that teaches through disruption
usage_example: Loki's tricks always cost something — but they also always reveal something

meaning: the realm of the quiet dead; she who receives the unchosen
usage_example: Hel does not judge — she simply holds

meaning: the sea-Vanir; master of tides, ships, and abundance
usage_example: Njörðr calms the waters for those who ask with respect

meaning: the Vanir lord of fertility, light, and peace
usage_example: Freyr does not rush spring — he invites it

meaning: the one-handed god of law, sacrifice, and justice
usage_example: Tyr gave his hand so the world might be bound — justice has a cost

meaning: the All-Father; wanderer, war-god, poet, and seeker
usage_example: Oðinn gave his eye not for power but for wisdom

meaning: keeper of the golden apples of renewal
usage_example: Íðunn tends what would otherwise grow old without purpose

meaning: the mountain-goddess of winter, hunting, and independence
usage_example: Skaði does not compromise her nature for comfort

meaning: the skald-god; divine patron of poetry and eloquence
usage_example: Let Bragi guide the tongue when mere words feel too small

meaning: the primordial cow who nourished the first giant
usage_example: Even Auðumbla knew — creation begins with sustenance

meaning: the first giant; the body of the cosmos made flesh
usage_example: From Ymir's bones the mountains; from his blood the sea

meaning: the ash tree — humanity's male progenitor
usage_example: We are children of Ask and Embla — given breath to know wonder

meaning: the elm — humanity's female progenitor
usage_example: In Embla's nature: capacity for form and feeling

meaning: mystery made visible; a letter that is also a door
usage_example: Every rune is a question the cosmos asks of you

meaning: cattle-rune; the rune of flow, wealth, and beginnings
usage_example: Fehu reminds: what flows must also move on

meaning: the aurochs-rune; wild strength and primal vitality
usage_example: Uruz does not ask permission to be powerful

meaning: the thorn-rune; protection, challenge, and piercing truth
usage_example: Thurisaz stands at the boundary — cross only with intention

meaning: the god-rune; divine breath, communication, and inspiration
usage_example: Ansuz opens the throat so truth may pass through

meaning: the journey-rune; the right path, rhythm, and movement
usage_example: Raidho asks: are you moving by choice or by inertia?

meaning: the torch-rune; craftsmanship, knowledge, and controlled fire
usage_example: Kenaz lights the interior dark where Fehu cannot reach

meaning: the gift-rune; balance in exchange, sacred reciprocity
usage_example: Gebo binds two into one web without erasing either

meaning: the joy-rune; the satisfaction of belonging and flourishing
usage_example: Wunjo is not euphoria — it is the deep quiet joy of being in the right place

meaning: the hail-rune; disruption that carries transformation
usage_example: Hagalaz destroys so that something truer may emerge

meaning: the need-rune; necessity, constraint, and endurance
usage_example: Nauthiz teaches: the fire is found by rubbing the need-staves together

meaning: the ice-rune; stillness, concentration, and the frozen moment
usage_example: Isa asks you to hold still and wait for the thaw

meaning: the year-rune; cycles, harvest, and the law of return
usage_example: Jera does not hurry — seasons cannot be rushed

meaning: the yew-rune; endurance, initiation, and the axis of the worlds
usage_example: Eihwaz stands at the center of every transformation

meaning: the dice-cup rune; chance, fate, and the hidden revealed
usage_example: Perthro spills what the cup was holding — be ready

meaning: the elk-rune; protection, reaching, and sacred defense
usage_example: Elhaz shields not by walls but by presence

meaning: the sun-rune; victory, wholeness, and the guiding light
usage_example: Sowilo does not hide — it illuminates and moves on

meaning: the tyr-rune; justice, sacrifice, and unwavering direction
usage_example: Tiwaz points north regardless of the weather

meaning: the birch-rune; birth, growth, becoming, and tender new things
usage_example: Berkano asks: what is still too new to rush?

meaning: the horse-rune; cooperation, partnership, and swift movement
usage_example: Ehwaz is not one rider or one horse — it is the perfect union of both

meaning: the human-rune; self-awareness, community, and shared humanity
usage_example: Mannaz sees the self reflected in the other

meaning: the water-rune; the unconscious, flow, and the unseen depths
usage_example: Laguz does not resist — it finds its own way around every obstacle

meaning: the seed-rune; potential, gestation, and internal completion
usage_example: Ingwaz holds the whole tree folded inside the seed

meaning: the ancestral-rune; inheritance, sacred enclosure, belonging
usage_example: Othala is the place that claims you even when you wander

meaning: the dawn-rune; breakthrough, balance between opposites, transformation
usage_example: Dagaz is the threshold between the night-self and the day-self

meaning: the skald's speech — heightened poetic language
usage_example: Let skáld-mál carry what ordinary words cannot hold

meaning: wholeness, hale — health of body and soul together
usage_example: May you be heill in all the ways that matter

meaning: a wand or magical staff; the channelled will made tangible
usage_example: The gandr knows which way to point before the hand decides

meaning: the fire within — passion, drive, and sacred fury
usage_example: The eldr that burns without consuming is the rarest kind

meaning: home-world; the realm one has made one's own
usage_example: Wherever you find frith, there is heimr

meaning: a sacred enclosure; a sanctified space set apart
usage_example: Between us there is a vé — enter with reverence

meaning: a sacred sacrifice or offering to honor the holy powers
usage_example: Every true effort is a form of blót

meaning: prophetic vision; the sight that pierces the veil of time
usage_example: She spoke not from guessing but from spá

meaning: spirit-locking songs sung to call the helper spirits
usage_example: The vardlokkur must be sung with the full body, not just the voice

meaning: sitting out alone in darkness to invite vision
usage_example: The útiseta requires patience older than thought

meaning: stone; the ancient, unmoving witness of all things
usage_example: The grjót remembers what we have already forgotten

meaning: the assembly; where all voices meet to find the true path
usage_example: No þing reaches wisdom without honest speaking

meaning: custom; the honored ways handed down through the kin
usage_example: We do not follow siðr blindly but in understanding

meaning: dishonor; the mark left by cowardice or broken oath
usage_example: The níð of one untruth ripples through all future words

meaning: female ancestor-spirits who protect the bloodline
usage_example: The dísir watch from beyond the threshold of the living

meaning: chooser of the slain; she who decides who rises
usage_example: The valkyrja does not mourn — she chooses with clarity

meaning: the honored dead who feast in Valhöll until Ragnarök
usage_example: Every great deed earns a seat among the einherjar

meaning: the hall of the chosen — glory and perpetual becoming
usage_example: Valhöll is not just a reward — it is a state of readiness

meaning: the realm of the Aesir; the city of divine order
usage_example: Ásgarðr is built on sacrifice, not comfort

meaning: middle-world; the realm of humans and ordinary wonder
usage_example: Miðgarðr asks only that we live it fully

meaning: the outer world beyond safe boundaries; the wild
usage_example: The útgarðr is dangerous and necessary in equal measure

meaning: the world of mist and ice; primordial cold
usage_example: From Niflheim and Múspellsheim came the first becoming

meaning: the realm of fire; primordial heat that shapes all things
usage_example: Múspell holds the warmth that Niflheim needs to birth something

meaning: the realm of the dark elves and dwarven smiths
usage_example: The greatest crafts come from Svartálfaheimr — made in the deep dark

meaning: the light-elf realm; radiant, liminal, and finely beautiful
usage_example: Álfheimr light does not blind — it makes everything more itself

meaning: the giant-world; untamed elemental force and ancient wisdom
usage_example: Jötunheimr is not evil — it is simply older than our categories

meaning: the Vanir realm; nature magic, fertility, and deep time
usage_example: Vanaheimr knows things Ásgarðr has yet to learn

meaning: the duel on the island; facing the thing that must be faced
usage_example: Some conflicts only end in hólmganga — look it in the eye

meaning: the bear-warrior; one who enters the trance of absolute action
usage_example: The berserkr does not think — the berserkr becomes

meaning: the wolf-warrior; one who wears the wolf's skin into battle
usage_example: Úlfhéðinn fights not for glory but because the wolf will not be still

meaning: a runic inscription; a carved intention made permanent
usage_example: Some words are not spoken — they are carved as staves

meaning: the outer-world trickster; master of illusion and scale
usage_example: Útgarðaloki reminds: what you think you are fighting may not be the real thing

meaning: Oðin's eight-legged horse; he who crosses between all worlds
usage_example: Sleipnir moves where others cannot follow

meaning: the great wolf bound until Ragnarök; the power that cannot be contained forever
usage_example: Every truth eventually breaks free like Fenrir

meaning: the Midgård Serpent; the boundary that encircles all things
usage_example: Jörmungandr teaches: what encircles you is also what you contain

meaning: the corpse-gnawer at Yggdrasil's roots; entropy as teacher
usage_example: Nidhoggr reminds us that nothing is permanent — and that is not wrong

meaning: the squirrel who carries messages up and down the World Tree
usage_example: Ratatoskr ensures that what is said at the root reaches the crown

meaning: tooth-gnasher — one of Thor's goats; daily renewed vitality
usage_example: Like Tanngrisnir and Tanngnjóstr — what is consumed is reborn each morning

meaning: tooth-grinder — the second of Thor's goats; relentless strength
usage_example: Tanngnjóstr grinds through what Tanngrisnir leaves behind

meaning: the dwarf whose cursed gold begins a great tragedy; caution around greed
usage_example: Andvarr's gold teaches: some gifts carry their own undoing

meaning: the dragon-slayer; courage meeting the primal fear
usage_example: Every Sigurðr must face their Fáfnir alone

meaning: the shieldmaiden surrounded by fire; love and sovereignty
usage_example: Brynhildr waited not from weakness but from absolute standards

meaning: Oðin's spear; the infallible aim of sworn intention
usage_example: Gungnir never misses — decide carefully what you aim at

meaning: Thor's hammer; the thunderous return to right order
usage_example: Mjölnir does not arbitrate — it resolves

meaning: Oðin's arm-ring that drips eight new rings every nine nights
usage_example: Draupnir teaches that genuine abundance multiplies itself

meaning: a cunning dwarf; the one who guards treasure in disguise
usage_example: Not all guardians announce themselves

meaning: grey — the liminal color between black and white, certainty and mystery
usage_example: Most truths live in the grá

meaning: sun-wheel; the eternal cycle of light and return
usage_example: The sól-hjúl turns regardless of our readiness

meaning: the Sayings of the High One — Oðin's wisdom poetry
usage_example: The Hávamál says: better to give one gift than to beg

meaning: the great mythological compilations; the memory of the cosmos
usage_example: The eddur preserve what would otherwise dissolve into forgetting

meaning: a true tale; what the ancestors lived and passed forward
usage_example: Your saga is being written whether you watch it or not

meaning: Thor's wood; sacred forest valley between glaciers
usage_example: þórsmörk — where even silence has a name

meaning: land-spirit; the invisible guardian of a particular place
usage_example: One must greet the landvættr before assuming a place is safe

meaning: an elf; a being of radiant, non-human vitality
usage_example: The alfr moves through the visible world but belongs to another

meaning: the dwarves; master-crafters born from the first giant's decay
usage_example: The dvergar make what the gods cannot — beauty from necessity

meaning: a giant; raw elemental power that precedes civilization
usage_example: The þurs is not evil — it simply has no interest in your comfort

meaning: the river of the dead; boundary water between worlds
usage_example: Vaðgelmir flows where all things eventually arrive

meaning: the dark fields; realm of ancient stone and deep work
usage_example: Niðavellir is where the unfinished business of the world is hammered into shape

meaning: the field of the folk — Freyja's hall where half the chosen dead dwell
usage_example: Fólkvangr is no lesser paradise than Valhöll — simply a different kind of becoming

meaning: Freyja's great hall; the spacious one, welcoming all who are worthy
usage_example: Sessrúmnir has room for grief as well as joy

meaning: the goat who grazes on Yggdrasil and gives endless mead
usage_example: Heiðrún is the paradox of plenty — the more given, the more there is

meaning: the stag on Valhöll's roof whose antlers drip into the world's rivers
usage_example: Eikþyrnir feeds the rivers that feed all living things — unseen generosity

meaning: shining-mane — the horse of day who lights the sky as he runs
usage_example: Skinfaxi does not wonder if his mane is bright enough — he simply runs

meaning: frost-mane — the horse of night who drops foam as dew on the fields
usage_example: Hrímfaxi passes in silence; he marks his way in morning moisture

meaning: the first nourisher; she who licked the salt-ice to reveal the first god
usage_example: Auðhumla teaches: nourishment shapes what it touches into something greater

meaning: the first god, revealed from ice by Auðhumla's tongue
usage_example: Búri was not made — he was revealed

meaning: Búri's son; father of Oðin, Víli, and Vé
usage_example: Borr is the link between the primal and the divine — the necessary middle generation

meaning: will — the second of Oðin's brothers who gave will to the first humans
usage_example: Víli gave us will — the capacity to choose and bear the consequence

meaning: the sacred — the third brother who gave sense and speech to the first humans
usage_example: Vé gave us language — the bridge between inner and outer

meaning: transgression of gender norms; sometimes a power, sometimes an accusation
usage_example: Ergi is called a curse by the fearful and a gift by the wise

meaning: a seeress; a woman who speaks what the spirits show her
usage_example: The spakonur does not pretend certainty — she speaks what she sees

meaning: a woman who works seiðr; one who weaves fate directly
usage_example: The seiðkona does not merely predict fate — she shapes it

meaning: a rune-wise one; a man who works deep knowledge
usage_example: The vitki spends years becoming worthy of what the runes offer

meaning: raven — the bird of memory, omen, and divine intelligence
usage_example: Where the hrafn circles, the veil grows thin

meaning: wolf — the creature of wildness, loyalty, and the pack
usage_example: The úlfr does not explain its loyalty — it simply gives it completely

meaning: bear — the solitary, powerful one who knows when to be still
usage_example: The björn does not need permission to take up space

meaning: serpent or dragon — wisdom through transformation and shedding
usage_example: The ormr knows that what you shed was never truly you

meaning: eagle — the vision that sees from above; the wide view
usage_example: The örn does not hesitate before the dive — it simply knows

meaning: stag — grace, renewal, and the seasonal turn of things
usage_example: The hjörtr sheds his antlers knowing they will return greater

meaning: golden — suffused with inner and outer radiance
usage_example: There are moments that are simply gullinn — and they are enough

meaning: fair, beautiful — the quality of things that are as they should be
usage_example: Fagr is not decoration — it is the signature of rightness

meaning: steadfast of mind; unwavering in the face of pressure
usage_example: Be hugfast when the noise grows loud

meaning: counsellor — one who speaks true advice from genuine care
usage_example: A ráðgjafi speaks the truth even when it costs them

meaning: the two horses of night and day; the necessary pair
usage_example: The eykr remind us: opposites do not cancel but complete

meaning: the void of creative potential before creation
usage_example: Every new beginning is a small ginnungagap — formless possibility

meaning: fair of face and good of character together
usage_example: Mannfagr is rare — beauty that is not a mask

meaning: vigor and excellence; the quality of doing the thing well
usage_example: þróttr is not about winning — it is about the quality brought to the attempt

meaning: clan-guardian spirit; the invisible force that protects the family line
usage_example: The kynfylgja travels with the blood, not the house

meaning: folk-magic; practical enchantment woven from the everyday
usage_example: Trolldom lives in the kitchen as much as the forest

meaning: black — the color of the void, initiation, and unlit possibility
usage_example: Svartr is not the absence of light — it is the presence of all that light reveals

meaning: white — the color of bone, snow, beginnings, and the clear view
usage_example: Hvítr is not innocence — it is clarity

meaning: red — the color of blood, life, courage, and the vital force
usage_example: Rauðr runs through all living things

meaning: dark-blue — the color of the sea, the sky, and the bruise of change
usage_example: Bláinn is the color of depth — it does not panic in the dark

meaning: green — the color of the growing world and the patient return of life
usage_example: Grœnn reminds: dormancy is not death

meaning: endurance; the quality of holding through without breaking
usage_example: þrekr is not stubbornness — it is the choice to continue with intention

meaning: the companionship of warriors; bonds forged in shared ordeal
usage_example: Vígsmannalag is the love that does not need explaining between those who have stood together

meaning: many-skilled magic; the lore that spans the worlds
usage_example: Fjölkyngi is not one power but the weaving of many

meaning: understood; the quality of being seen and known completely
usage_example: To be gáðr is rarer than to be praised

meaning: life itself; the particular spark that animates the body
usage_example: Líf is not a resource to be managed — it is a gift to be spent well

meaning: the heart — the true seat of courage and feeling
usage_example: What the hjarta knows, the head eventually admits

meaning: gentle of mind; calm intelligence without harshness
usage_example: Hugur-blíðr is the quality of speaking difficulty with kindness

meaning: a servant-companion; one who grows by serving something worthy
usage_example: The best þjálfr knows that the service teaches them more than the master

meaning: love-mind; the heart tuned to the frequency of deep care
usage_example: Elskuhugr sees the worth in what others overlook

meaning: folk, people — the web of all who share a world and its concerns
usage_example: The þýðr endures what the individual cannot

meaning: courageous, high-spirited — the quality of facing without flinching
usage_example: Móðugr is not the absence of fear but the decision to proceed regardless

meaning: the natural grace of one's kind; the ease of being what you are
usage_example: Kynþokki cannot be learned — it blooms when the self is accepted

meaning: soulful; possessing depth and the capacity for full presence
usage_example: Sálingi is the quality the runes seek in those who work them

meaning: wise and learned; enriched by both knowledge and experience
usage_example: Fróðr is the quiet authority of one who has actually lived the lessons

meaning: mercy, grace — the gift given freely to the one who needed it
usage_example: Líkn is not weakness — it requires more courage than judgment

meaning: a poetic name; a title that reveals the true nature of a thing
usage_example: Every being worthy of a heiti has been seen truly

meaning: to know one another; the process of genuine mutual recognition
usage_example: þekkjast does not happen in a day — it is the work of many seasons
